programmes.show_description: There is talk of a wildcat strike among drivers - if any Hatley staff join it, the station could be closed. S2 E5: Lucky Strike
Gary returns to the bar to begin courting Phoebe, where he runs into her husband Donald. S1 E2: Fools Rush In
Mr. Bean goes to a swimming pool but struggles with the diving board, attempts to leave a car park in style and demonstrates some sandwich-making. E3: The Curse of Mr. Bean
Armed robberies lead the CID unit to prison, to escort safe-cracker Dickie Fingers for interview. They are set upon by a gang determined to get their hands on Fingers. Starring John Simm. S2 E2
Sandra and Beryl stay downstairs in Gerry's flat while he is away to help Sandra and her new husband get some time alone, but Gerry comes back early with his mum. S2 E5: The Good Samaritans
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
Brenda and Malcolm go for a spot of bird watching in Malcolm's hide, but Malcolm ends up flat on his back when the hide collapses; meanwhile, Oswald appears again in his cafe. S1 E7: Hiding
Tracey is out of prison, and Sharon gets a job as a school dinner supervisor and recruits Garth as her cook and Tracey as her washer-up. S11 E6: The Chief, The Cook, His Mum and Her Lodger
Mr. Bean loses midnight on New Year's Eve, devises an unusual method of transporting his sales shopping home, and wreaks havoc by doing a spot of decorating. E9: Do-It-Yourself, Mr. Bean
When a young English couple arrive at the hotel, Amy and Linda assume that they are newlyweds because they are so evidently in love. S2 E3: Couples

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E4: When Love Walks In
Linda is delighted when her heartthrob - soap actor Frazer Hines - books into the hotel, but he only has eyes for Amy. S2 E4: El Astro
The Bishop is collecting for his charity auction, Lady Lavender throws her money out of the window, and light-fingered Alf - resourceful as ever - has a plan. S2 E3 Money Talks
An election polarises the household - Rigsby supports the Conservatives, Alan and Philip lean towards Labour, and Miss Jones is impressed with the Liberal candidate. S1 E7: Stand Up and Be Counted
When Dad throws Wolfie and Ken out from their home, they sneak back in later as squatters. S3 E5: We Shall Not Be Moved
Mr Boswell's court date clashes with the day Billy and Julie have secretly decided to get married. S4 E1
Sandra needs the flat to entertain her new boyfriend Danny, meaning that Beryl pretends to be with her boyfriend but is visiting Gerry in the flat downstairs instead. S2 E4: The Proposal
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
The Bishop is collecting for his charity auction, Lady Lavender throws her money out of the window, and light-fingered Alf - resourceful as ever - has a plan. S2 E3 Money Talks

When Mildred enters a flower arranging competition, Chrissy offers her a plant from her garden; when Larry sees one in Chrissy's flat, he thinks it's a cannabis plant. S5 E5: How Does Your Garden Grow
Mr Jeremy Brown, an English teacher, meets his class of foreign students for the first time. Things start off well, but... S1 E1: The First Lesson
Gwenneth and Min come to visit Alf. They decide to hold a seance to talk to long departed friends. S3 E4
Sandra needs the flat to entertain her new boyfriend Danny, meaning that Beryl pretends to be with her boyfriend but is visiting Gerry in the flat downstairs instead. S2 E4: The Proposal
Mr Boswell's court date clashes with the day Billy and Julie have secretly decided to get married. S4 E1
Arthur comes back to earth with a bump when he learns that he faces tough opposition for the Daley inheritance. S9 E12: A Taste of Money
Ethel has fallen in love with an American pilot called Joe, but Wilfred is not at all pleased. S2 E6: Love is a Very Splendid Thing
Gary arouses suspicions when he saves Phoebe from an early evening bombing raid. S1 E3: Is Your Journey Really Necessary?
Mr. Bean buys a TV and a new camera but has issues with both; meanwhile, he ends up in another pickle when he goes to a magic show and a disco. E4: Mr. Bean Goes to Town
Drama series about a cop who wakes up in what seems to be 1973. After a bomb warning, A-Division go on red alert, but it doesn't fit with Sam's understanding of IRA methods. Starring John Simm. S2 E3
Sandra's friend, a posh beautiful actress, comes to stay but soon puts Beryl's nose out of joint when she takes an interest with both of their dates for a night. S2 E6: Three's a Crowd
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!
It is nearly Christmas; Malcolm and Pamela go to get a tree, but plans for Christmas Day don't go as hoped for so Brenda and Malcolm find their own solution. S1 E8: Seasoning
Sharon makes plans to take Tracey and Dorien on a madcap '70s weekender, but there is no dressing up soe news that Tracey is about to receive. S11 E7: Without a Trace
Mr. Bean goes to the funfair and ends up having to watch over a baby; will the mother find them in time? E10: Mind the Baby, Mr. Bean

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S4, E5: An Open-And-Shut Case
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe
Lady Lavender's money goes astray and she calls in the police. Will her money turn up at the bishop's auction inside one of the Meldrum vases? S2 E4 The Meldrum Vases
When Ruth is depressed following the 'deception' of a recent male friend, Rigsby takes what he believes to be his chance to woo her. S2 E1: The Permissive Society
While performing at the pub, Wolfie attracts the attention of the wealthy Caroline who invites him to her aristocratic father's house. S3 E6: The Party's Over
It's the big day for Billy and Julie at the Registrar's office and for Freddie at the Court House. Cousin Eddie arrives from Canada. S4 E2
Sandra and Beryl stay downstairs in Gerry's flat while he is away to help Sandra and her new husband get some time alone, but Gerry comes back early with his mum. S2 E5: The Good Samaritans
The annual bowls club outing seems to provide an ideal opportunity for Cecil to be alone with May. S2 E7: A Bowl In The Hand
Lady Lavender's money goes astray and she calls in the police. Will her money turn up at the bishop's auction inside one of the Meldrum vases? S2 E4 The Meldrum Vases
Chrissy receives two tickets for a Frank Sinatra concert but does not want them; she instead enjoys being pampered by Jo and Robin as they compete for the tickets. S5 E6: Come Fly With Me
Ms. Courtney informs Mr. Brown that an education inspector will be arriving, as will a new African student. These two arrivals create a confusing lesson. S1 E2: Inspector Calls
Alf has a fight at a tea dance and arranges to meet up again with his adversary outside his house. S3 E5

Penniless and facing the threat of deportation from Australia, Ray and Arthur must try to raise funds for a return airfare home. S9 E13: For a Few Dollars More
